title: hoya odorata subsp. miquilingensis
intro: Hoya odorata subsp. miquilingensis is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to the Philippines. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, headache, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits like reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. The herbal preparation of Hoya odorata subsp. miquilingensis typically involves infusing the leaves in hot water to make a tea, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
